# Trusted Runtime Envelope

<!-- Generated by go run ./tools/providerruntimeresponsibilitydocs -write-doc. Do not edit by hand. -->

[Back to runtime responsibility](../runtime-responsibility.md)

A trusted runtime envelope is a provider-specific process shape that has explicit authority and conformance tests.

- Codex currently adopts the full-access app-server envelope owned by daemon-generated arguments.
- Claude requires explicit PermissionMode and rejects unsafe bypass unless policy allows the isolated trust tier.
- Cursor and OpenClaw remain unpromoted until equivalent command, permission, and integration evidence exists.

Promotion requires security review, command-shape tests, and provider adapter conformance evidence.
